name: band one arm twisting chest press
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at chest height.
Stand with your side facing the anchor point and grab the band with one hand.
Step away from the anchor point to create tension in the band.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Bring your hand holding the band across your body, towards the opposite shoulder.
While maintaining tension in the band, push your hand forward and away from your body, extending your arm.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other hand.

Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at chest height. Stand with your side facing the anchor point and grab the band with one hand. Step away from the anchor point to create tension in the band. Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees. Bring your hand holding the band across your body, towards the opposite shoulder. While maintaining tension in the band, push your hand forward and away from your body, extending your arm. Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other hand.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
